summary: Ukrainian FPV drones struck the Afipsky oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai on March 14, 2026, causing a massive fire confirmed by NASA's FIRMS monitoring service. Russian forces used the "Yolka" anti-drone system to down Ukrainian Air Force fixed-wing "Hornet" UAVs, while Ukrainian operators of the "Rubikon" system reported 136 targets eliminated across the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions on the same day. Ukrainian infantry, after failed surrender negotiations with Russian survivors, called in FPV drones to eliminate the remaining enemy. Ukrainian FPV pilots employed tactics such as dismounting Russian soldiers from horses before strikes and simultaneously targeting infantry with multiple drones. A Russian soldier was recorded charging an FPV drone with a bayonet and being destroyed, and another was hit by two Ukrainian FPVs while hiding behind a tree. Long-range fiber-optic FPV drones continued to destroy Russian artillery positions, with crews unable to effectively conceal or defend their systems.
